title: Hidden Kyoto
subtitle: Alleyways & Artisans
description: Kyoto's most celebrated streets are beautiful. But the real city lives in the narrow lanes between them — in the workshops of craftspeople, in the hidden shrines, in the neighbours who still greet each other by name. This full-day experience takes you off every map you've seen.
highlights: Private walking tour of Kyoto's hidden alleyways
Visit to a working craftsman's studio (ceramics or lacquerware)
Hidden neighbourhood shrine, rarely visited by tourists
Lunch at a family-run machiya restaurant
Personal guide fluent in English and deeply familiar with local history

itinerary.activity: Meet your guide at Kyoto Station
Morning walk through Nishiki & hidden side streets
Visit to artisan workshop — watch the craft, ask questions
Lunch in a century-old machiya townhouse
Explore the less-visited northern lanes of Gion
Hidden Shinto shrine — your guide explains the meaning behind it
Evening sake tasting at a neighbourhood bar
Tour ends — you leave knowing the city differently

title: Private Tea Ceremony
subtitle: With a Tea Master
description: Tea ceremony is not a performance. It is, at its core, a practice of presence — of giving your full attention to a single moment. In a private machiya away from the tourist circuit, a tea master with decades of experience will guide you through this quietly transformative ritual.
highlights: Private ceremony in an authentic Kyoto machiya
Guided by a practising tea master with 20+ years of experience
Seasonal wagashi sweets crafted by a local confectioner
Full explanation of the philosophy and history of chado
Small group only — maximum 6 guests for an intimate experience

itinerary.activity: Arrive at the machiya — seasonal garden welcome
Introduction to chado philosophy and the tea room
Observe the tea master's preparation ritual
Participate in your own ceremony, guided step by step
Quiet reflection and conversation with the tea master
Experience ends

title: Dawn at Fushimi Inari
subtitle: Before the World Wakes
description: Fushimi Inari is one of Japan's most photographed sites. By 9am it is crowded. By 7am, it belongs to you. We begin before sunrise, walking the torii gates in near-silence, with a guide who can explain what they mean — not just what they look like.
highlights: Pre-dawn start to beat all crowds
Walk the full inner mountain trail, not just the lower section
Guide explains Shinto symbolism and the history of Inari worship
Sunrise over Kyoto from the mountain viewpoints
Hot tea prepared at the summit

itinerary.activity: Meet your guide at Fushimi Inari station
Enter the shrine at first light
Reach the inner mountain trails — almost no one here
Sunrise from Yotsutsuji viewpoint
Summit tea and quiet reflection
Descend and explore the lower shrines
Experience ends

title: Local Food Journey
subtitle: Markets, Stalls & Stories
description: Kyoto's food culture is quieter than Osaka, but it runs deeper. There are dishes here that have barely changed in 400 years. We'll take you to the places that have no signage in English — the pre-dawn fish market, the tofu shop that opens at 6am, the ramen bar where the owner is also the cook, the waiter, and the dishwasher.
highlights: Early morning visit to Nishiki Market before the crowds
Breakfast at a local teishoku restaurant (set breakfast, no English menu)
Visit to a 150-year-old tofu producer
Lunch at a counter ramen bar with your guide
Sake tasting at a neighbourhood liquor shop

itinerary.activity: Meet at Nishiki Market east entrance
Early morning tour of the market — tastings along the way
Breakfast at a local teishoku restaurant
Tofu producer visit — see how it's made, taste it fresh
Walk to a neighbourhood lunch spot — ramen or soba
Sake tasting to close
Experience ends

title: Gion After Dark
subtitle: The Floating World
description: The lanterns along Hanamikoji come on just after dusk. In summer the air is warm and the cicadas are quiet. In autumn the stone paving reflects the light perfectly. A walk through Gion at this hour, with someone who understands what you're seeing, is one of the most memorable things you can do in Japan.
highlights: Evening walk through Gion's most atmospheric streets
Guide explains the history and realities of the geisha world
Visit to a specialist bar in a historic machiya
Optional kaiseki dinner reservation at a private restaurant
Opportunity to see maiko and geiko on their evening walks

itinerary.activity: Meet your guide near Gion Shijo
Walk through Hanamikoji as the lanterns light up
Explore the hidden lanes of Shirakawa and Tatsumi Shrine
Stop at a machiya bar — whisky or sake, your choice
Final walk through the quieter northern lanes
Experience ends

title: Zen & Silence
subtitle: Temple Meditation
description: Kyoto has over 1,600 temples. Most of them you walk through in 20 minutes and feel nothing. This experience is different. We work with a Zen monk at a temple that does not advertise. You sit. You walk. You rake gravel. You leave having understood something you couldn't have understood before.
highlights: Private meditation session with a resident Zen monk
Walking meditation (kinhin) in the temple gardens
Raked gravel garden — participate in the practice
Guided explanation of Zen philosophy in plain language
Temple moss garden walk, usually closed to the public

itinerary.activity: Arrive at the temple before morning visitors
Introduction to seated meditation (zazen)
Walking meditation through the garden
Gravel raking practice
Conversation with the monk — open questions
Walk through the private moss garden
Experience ends
